title: ED vomiting documentation

presenting_history.text: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact DHA assessment section supports onset, duration, severity or functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient concerns for nausea or vomiting without inferring the cause.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

red_flags.text: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician risk context, manual vital-sign and focused assessment, severity, appearance and time-critical findings only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

red_flags.text: Lists urgent referral features including haematemesis, neurological symptoms, chest pain, acute abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, and immunosuppression.
red_flags.rationale: The exact referral section supports clinician-decided escalation, plan, completed specialist discussion and arranged handover without generating referral or treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists urgent referral features including haematemesis, neurological symptoms, chest pain, acute abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, and immunosuppression.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

investigations.text: States that testing is history-directed and lists glucose, blood count, inflammatory markers, electrolytes, stool culture, and urinalysis.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording existing results or imaging only when already ordered, performed or reviewed; it does not recommend testing.
investigations.evidence_extract: States that testing is history-directed and lists glucose, blood count, inflammatory markers, electrolytes, stool culture, and urinalysis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

source_limitations: Episode count, contents, hydration, intake, pregnancy, exposures, abdominal or neurological examination, test values, diagnosis, treatment, medication and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
